title: eleusine gracilis
intro: Eleusine gracilis, also known as wild millet or panic grass, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Eleusine gracilis has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
